title: Data Definition Language (DDL) and Data Manipulation Language (DML)
summary: 9618 AS SQL — CREATE, SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, and relational query logic for Paper 1 and Paper 3.
learningObjectives: Write SELECT queries with WHERE, ORDER BY, and basic joins.
Use INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE correctly.
Interpret query results from given tables.

simpleExplanation.title: Talking to the database
simpleExplanation.summary: DDL defines structure (CREATE TABLE); DML changes or reads data (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE). SQL is set-based — one statement can affect many rows.
steps: SELECT field FROM table WHERE condition ORDER BY field;
INSERT INTO table VALUES (...); or specify columns.
UPDATE table SET field = value WHERE condition;
JOIN links tables on matching key fields.

sections.content: **SQL** (8.3) is tested on Paper 1 theory and Paper 3 practical. Practise **trace tables** for UPDATE/DELETE and **join** two normalised tables.

sections.question: List student names enrolled on course C101, sorted A–Z.
sections.solution:
SELECT Student.Name
FROM Student
INNER JOIN Enrolment ON Student.StudentID = Enrolment.StudentID
WHERE Enrolment.CourseCode = 'C101'
ORDER BY Student.Name;
